reason: Enabling FIPS mode requires adding a kernel boot parameter (fips=1) and changing the system-wide cryptographic policy. This change is not supported on an already installed system and can render the system unbootable or cause widespread cryptographic failures, leading to loss of access. The policy explicitly classifies FIPS changes as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; authentication/authorization affected; Potential boot failure; System lockout; Broad impact on cryptographic operations; Incompatibility with existing installed system
